title: Fatigue degrades orders before firepower
discovery: Under prolonged stress, the first thing to fail is not the gun; it is judgment, sequencing, and the ability to keep a coherent plan in mind.
evidence: source_library/40_combat-stress-reaction.md:15-17 - fatigue becomes operationally visible through stress reaction and unit cohesion loss.
source_library/41_united_states_army_air_forces.md:15-17 - the Army used rotation policy because exhaustion had real effectiveness costs.
source_library/50_army_ground_forces.md:15-17 - replacements and training are the institutional answer to degraded effectiveness.
why: This suggests a command-degradation system where options, sequencing, and recall get worse before raw combat ability fully collapses.
mechanic: Progressive command degradation, not just stat penalties.
pressure: Tired crews produce worse plans, slower reaction, and more mistakes than healthy crews.
